codeReview.summary: Implementation satisfies the feature contract: package scope references in package.json and TS/TSX imports were migrated to @papierklammer, lockfile was regenerated, and verification tests were added. Commit-level spot checks found no stale @paperclipai package/import references outside intentional test search strings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rename-worker procedure assumes ripgrep (`rg`) is available for verification, but this environment does not have rg installed, so the worker had to deviate to find/grep fallbacks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rename-worker/SKILL.md step 3 requires rg; worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 8f84d95b-5736-4020-8b7b-5729c14d6e80 shows find/grep fallback; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges calls this out explicitly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: ENOSPC-related test instability was encountered during this feature run, but this local environment caveat is not captured in shared environment knowledge.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues includes workspace-runtime ENOSPC note; transcript skeleton shows temp cleanup and low disk-space check before retrying tests.

summary: Pass. The feature implementation is consistent with VAL-FORK-001/VAL-FORK-002 and the stated expected behavior for package scope migration in package manifests and TypeScript imports.
